channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: US strikes new targets in Iran as Tehran hits Gulf states, Hormuz shipping
item.description: Iran's deputy foreign minister accuses US of destroying the interim peace deal.

item.title: US President Trump meets with ‘fan of America’ Iraqi PM Ali al-Zaidi
item.description: US President Donald Trump welcomed Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi to the White House.

item.title: Families claim bodies of victims of deadly Bangkok pub fire
item.description: ‘No one has been able to accept it yet.’

item.title: US risks falling into ‘escalation trap’ if it deploys troops in Iran
item.description: Analyst Alex Alfirraz Scheers says the US risks falling into an escalation trap if it launches a ground assault in Iran.

item.title: Trump pivots from 20 percent Strait of Hormuz fee amid Iran war escalation
item.description: Donald Trump backs away from fee, says regional countries will instead invest in US in return for transit protection.

item.title: US President Trump drops 20% Strait of Hormuz fee plan
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he has abandoned his proposed 20% fee on cargo transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: US, Russian astronauts launch into orbit for joint space mission
item.description: NASA chief Jared Isaacman made a rare visit to the Russia-operated Baikonur Cosmodrome to witness the launch.

item.title: Cuba’s power grid collapses again, triggering third blackout in 10 days
item.description: Millions lost power as Cuba’s fifth nationwide blackout of 2026 hit amid a US-imposed oil blockade.

item.title: Trump meets with Iraq prime minister at White House, vows ‘a lot of deals’
item.description: Iraqi prime minister says relations with US shifting from militaristic to economic, vows disarmament of factions.

item.title: Sudan faces escalating hunger crisis due to war and Hormuz disruption – WFP
item.description: Renewed conflict and tensions in the Strait of Hormuz have slowed fertiliser shipments, worsening hunger in Sudan.

item.title: US consumer prices drop in June as energy costs tumble
item.description: Inflation eased due to cheaper energy, but experts warn price increases may return after renewed US-Iran tensions.

item.title: Murder of UK politician Ann Widdecombe was ‘targeted attack’, police say
item.description: Investigators are looking into what motivated the killing and how much preparation was involved.

item.title: World leaders travel to Qatar to honour Father Emir
item.description: Leaders from around the world have been in Qatar to offer condolences to Emir Sheikh Tamim.

item.title: Mexico: Football Without Fear
item.description: As Mexico cohosts the World Cup, a mother confronts gender violence and seeks safety for her daughter through football.

item.title: Mexico begins legal action in US over deaths of its citizens in ICE custody
item.description: Mexico files complaints and sends cease-and-desist letters over the deaths of 17 nationals in ICE custody or raids.

item.title: Israeli attack on north Gaza police station kills eight
item.description: Gaza's interior ministry denounces 'horrific massacre' that killed station chief, other police personnel and a civilian.

item.title: France vs Spain LIVE: FIFA World Cup 2026 semifinal
item.description: Live coverage and text updates as France play Spain in a semifinal clash between the two tournament favourites.

item.title: Jude Bellingham Station unveiled ahead of World Cup semi-final
item.description: A station in southeast London has been temporarily renamed Jude Bellingham Station before the World Cup semi-finals.

item.title: Leading Houthi threatens ‘siege’ on Saudi Arabia after Yemen airport attack
item.description: Speaking to Al Jazeera, leading Houthi Mohammed al-Bukhaiti warns of attacks on Saudi Arabia.

item.title: How the late Father Emir transformed Qatar’s economy
item.description: How the late Father Emir transformed Qatar’s economy

item.title: Even if the Iran war ends, the next fossil fuel shock is already on its way
item.description: To prepare, the world needs to focus on building energy independence through renewables and decentralisation.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: Meet Ismail Elfath, referee for England-Argentina semifinal
item.description: American-Moroccan referee Elfath will take charge of England against Argentina, his fourth game at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: India summons Iranian diplomat over missile killing of seafarer
item.description: India summoned Iran's deputy ambassador after an Indian crew member was killed in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Olympic funding questioned by nine EU nations over Russia readmission
item.description: Nine EU nations urge funding cuts for sports bodies including International Olympic Committee over readmitting Russians.

item.title: ‘Piracy’: Will Trump’s 20 percent Hormuz toll find takers?
item.description: Trump says the US will start charging a cargo fee for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz.
